user_prompt: Describe Bernoulli’s principle through an airplane wing example in depth.

style_bible.visual_style: Clean educational CGI, realistic airplane wing with visible airflow streamlines, minimalistic overlays, neutral sky backdrop.
style_bible.camera_rules: Stable, readable compositions; avoid rapid motion; overlays only during static explanatory hold (no camera motion).
style_bible.lighting_rules: Soft directional daylight from upper-left, consistent across scenes, no dramatic shadows.
style_bible.continuity_rules: Same wing, same sky, same left-to-right airflow direction; Scene 2 begins exactly on Scene 1 final frame.

scenes.prompt:
SCENE INTENT: Introduce Bernoulli’s principle by showing airflow speed differences over an airplane wing. GLOBAL CONTINUITY: Primary subject is a single airplane wing viewed from the side; environment is open blue sky; camera maintains left-to-right screen direction, mid-distance; explanatory state builds calmly.
SHOT 1 (0–3s): framing: medium-wide side view of wing; lens: 35mm; camera movement: slow dolly; subject & action: by 1s airflow lines appear, by 2s they stabilize, by 3s wing cruises smoothly; on-screen text overlay: none; continuity note: airflow direction established for next shot; environment details: clear sky, faint clouds, distant horizon; lighting: soft daylight from upper-left; cinematic look: crisp, moderate depth of field.
SHOT 2 (3–7s): framing: same composition, slightly closer; lens: 50mm; camera movement: static explanatory hold (no camera motion); subject & action: airflow accelerates over curved top, calm and steady for reading; on-screen text overlay: "Curved top speeds flow"; continuity note: faster top airflow continues into next hold; environment details: blue sky gradient, subtle atmospheric haze; lighting: unchanged soft daylight; cinematic look: clean educational clarity.
SHOT 3 (7–10s): framing: unchanged; lens: 50mm; camera movement: static explanatory hold (no camera motion); subject & action: pressure visualization subtly fades in above wing, motion stable; on-screen text overlay: "Faster air, lower pressure"; continuity note: pressure shading remains into Scene 2; environment details: sky, airflow lines, wing surface; lighting: consistent soft light; cinematic look: smooth gradients. FINAL FRAME HANDOFF: Wing centered, airflow left-to-right, low-pressure shading visible above wing.
scenes.negative_prompt: logos, watermarks, brand marks, extra limbs, distorted faces, flicker, glitch, people, human, face, hands, cockpit, unreadable tiny text

scenes.prompt:
SCENE INTENT: Show how pressure difference creates lift and how angle affects it. GLOBAL CONTINUITY: Begins exactly from Scene 1 final frame with same wing, sky, airflow, camera distance, and screen direction; explanatory state continues.
SHOT 1 (0–3s): framing: identical to previous final frame; lens: 50mm; camera movement: slow dolly; subject & action: by 2s lift arrow gently rises from wing, motion calm; on-screen text overlay: none; continuity note: lift arrow grows clearer for next hold; environment details: blue sky, airflow lines, pressure shading; lighting: unchanged daylight; cinematic look: smooth educational CGI.
SHOT 2 (3–7s): framing: same; lens: 50mm; camera movement: static explanatory hold (no camera motion); subject & action: lift arrow steady, pressure difference clearly visible; on-screen text overlay: "Pressure difference creates lift"; continuity note: lift arrow remains for final shot; environment details: sky gradient, wing texture, airflow lines; lighting: soft and even; cinematic look: high clarity.
SHOT 3 (7–10s): framing: same with slight emphasis on leading edge; lens: 35mm; camera movement: static explanatory hold (no camera motion); subject & action: wing angle subtly tilts by 1–2 degrees, airflow adjusts smoothly, stable for reading; on-screen text overlay: "Angle affects lift"; continuity note: ends explanation cleanly with stable image; environment details: sky, airflow curvature, wing edge; lighting: consistent daylight; cinematic look: polished educational finish. FINAL FRAME HANDOFF: Wing angled slightly upward, airflow and lift arrow clearly visible, ready to end.
scenes.negative_prompt: logos, watermarks, brand marks, extra limbs, distorted faces, flicker, glitch, people, human, face, hands, cockpit, unreadable tiny text
scenes.camera: Locked side view, same as Scene 1

full_script: 0–3s: Establish airplane wing in flight with smooth airflow. 3–7s: Hold to show faster airflow over curved top; overlay appears: "Curved top speeds flow". 7–10s: Second hold clarifies pressure effect; overlay appears: "Faster air, lower pressure". 10–13s: Continue from same frame, reveal pressure difference with subtle color shading. 13–17s: Hold to explain lift creation; overlay appears: "Pressure difference creates lift". 17–20s: Final hold shows slight wing angle change affecting airflow; overlay appears: "Angle affects lift".
